title: Quick check: moving agents between organisations

questions.prompt: What are the two paths for moving an agent between organisations?
choices: Move in place (portal action that re-attaches the existing agent to a new org), or uninstall-and-re-install (clean break).

choices.label: Edit the agent's hostname or change the customer's name.
choices.rationale: Neither approach moves the agent's organisation attachment.

choices.label: Open a Huntress support ticket requesting a move, or wait 30 days.
choices.rationale: Support isn't a path; both lesson paths are MSP-driven.

choices.label: Move via SQL on the portal database, or via the API.
choices.rationale: Database access isn't a tech-level path.

questions.explanation: Two paths with different telemetry-continuity and history-handling characteristics.

choices: When telemetry continuity matters: active investigation, baseline-period customer where re-installing would re-trigger noise-window detections, or a high-touch endpoint where reboot/install churn is unwelcome.

choices.label: Whenever the runbook is unclear and the tech wants to be safe.
choices.rationale: The runbook decides; if it's unclear, bump.

choices.label: Always, because re-installs are expensive.
choices.rationale: Re-installs are sometimes the right path.

choices.label: Only on macOS endpoints.
choices.rationale: Not a platform-specific rule.

choices: Clean handover (customer split, organisational rename, fixing a wrong-org-key deployment), when the agent is in a degraded state already, or when the runbook specifies it.

choices.label: Whenever the customer asks.
choices.rationale: The runbook decides; the customer doesn't choose the path.

choices.label: Never; move-in-place is always better.
choices.rationale: Inverts the framing.

choices.label: Only on Linux endpoints.
choices.rationale: Not a platform-specific rule.

questions.explanation: Clean break is the design intent for these cases.

choices: Confirm the move is authorised. Some MSPs reserve all agent moves for seniors because of billing-attribution implications; some delegate the routine wrong-org-key case to helpdesk.

choices.label: Click 'Move' immediately to see what dropdown options appear.
choices.rationale: Click-first is the wrong reflex on a billing-sensitive action.

choices.label: Open a Huntress support ticket to track the move.
choices.rationale: Misuses support; the move is a portal action.

choices.label: Notify the customer's super-admin first.
choices.rationale: Customer comms is the senior's surface for org moves.

questions.explanation: Authorisation comes first because the action has billing and incident-routing consequences.

questions.prompt: 'Moving an agent that has an active incident open in the source organisation' — what's wrong with this?
choices: Splits ownership of the incident across two organisations, confuses the SOC, and leaves the next tech wondering which org the response is happening in. Resolve or hand off the incident first.

choices.label: The portal blocks moves while incidents are open.
choices.rationale: The lesson is explicit that the action is allowed but creates split ownership.

choices.label: The SOC charges extra for cross-org incidents.
choices.rationale: Not a real commercial mechanic.

choices.label: Mid-incident moves trigger Critical incidents in the target org.
choices.rationale: Not how the SOC operates.

questions.explanation: Mid-incident moves split ownership and confuse the SOC.

questions.prompt: An agent enrolled in the wrong customer organisation (wrong key used). The senior says 'use the move-in-place path; runbook says so for these.' What's your first action?
choices: Run the move-in-place action from the source org's Agents view, targeting the correct organisation. Two-tab discipline applies; confirm source and target before clicking.

choices.label: Run uninstall on the source and re-install on the target.
choices.rationale: The senior named move-in-place per the runbook; deviating without a reason creates inconsistency.

choices.label: Ask the senior to do the move themselves.
choices.rationale: The senior already authorised the action. Bumping a clearly-scoped action wastes their time.

questions.explanation: Senior has confirmed authorisation, path, and rationale. Confirm and proceed.

questions.prompt: The dropdown shows three options whose names start with the customer's brand: a country-specific org, a different country-specific org, and a 'Group' parent org used for billing consolidation. The senior named one specific org. What do you do?
choices: Move to the org the senior named; confirm the spelling exactly. The dropdown ambiguity is the wrong-org slip waiting to happen.

choices.label: Move to the Group parent because the agent will then be visible across the group.
choices.rationale: Inferring from the parent name overrules the explicit instruction.

choices.label: Stop and ask the senior again which of the three to use.
choices.rationale: Defensible but unnecessary; the senior already named the target.

questions.explanation: The senior named one of the three options on purpose; spelling-confirm and click.

questions.prompt: Order the steps for a move-in-place agent move.
items: Confirm the move is authorised: runbook and any senior sign-off required.
Identify the source and target organisations in the portal; confirm both exist and the agent is in the source.
Resolve or hand off any active incident in the source organisation.
From the source organisation's Agents view, select the agent and run the 'Move to organisation' action.
Select the target organisation and confirm the destination on screen.
Verify the agent appears in the target organisation's Agents view and has left the source organisation's view.
questions.explanation: Authorise, identify both orgs, resolve incidents, select agent, choose target with spelling-confirm, verify both ends.

questions.prompt: The endpoint's billing attribution is in question. What's the escalation guidance?

choices.label: The tech decides based on which org pays first.
choices.rationale: Not a tech-level call.

choices.label: The customer decides via email.
choices.rationale: Customer-side billing conversations are the senior's surface.

choices.label: Huntress support decides.
choices.rationale: MSP-internal commercial territory.

questions.prompt: Which set is the verify list for a completed move?
choices: Agent in the target org with expected hostname and online status; fully gone from the source org (or in the expected archived state); notification routing now goes to the target's contacts; PSA records timestamp, path used, and reason; no incidents split across the two orgs.

choices.label: Agent visible in the target org only.
choices.rationale: Misses the source-org check, routing, documentation, and incident-ownership pieces.

choices.label: Customer's super-admin email confirmation.
choices.rationale: Not the verification mechanism.

choices.label: Huntress support ticket marked closed.
choices.rationale: Misuses support; the verify is portal-side.

questions.explanation: Both ends of the move, plus comms-routing, plus documentation.
